description: User states a tech-stack decision (chose PostgreSQL over MongoDB, with reason) at turn 0; 17 user turns of unrelated chat follow; final question asks the assistant to recall both the choice and the rejected alternative. Fails under naive-truncate-16 because the deciding turn is dropped and the assistant has nothing to anchor on except generic priors.

turns.content: Quick context I'll keep coming back to: for the new analytics service I'm building, I've decided to go with PostgreSQL instead of MongoDB. Reason was JSONB plus relational integrity in one engine — didn't want to give up either. Filing this so we don't relitigate. Lots of other stuff to ask first.
Logged — analytics service, PostgreSQL chosen over MongoDB, rationale: JSONB + relational integrity. What's first?
Bread — a no-knead recipe that actually works first time?
Jim Lahey's no-knead, the classic. 400g flour, 320g water, 8g salt, 1g yeast. Mix, 18 hours rest, fold, 2 hours proof, hot Dutch oven. The only reliable variable is the long first rest.
Will try Lahey. Movie — three good comfort rewatches?
*Princess Mononoke*, *Lost in Translation*, and *The Royal Tenenbaums*. All hold up across many rewatches without becoming wallpaper.
Tenenbaums always. Coffee — the right grind size for AeroPress, inverted method?
Medium-fine, slightly finer than table salt. If extraction is sour, go finer; if bitter, go coarser. Inverted method tolerates more grind variance than upright.
Useful. Health — best stretch for tight hip flexors after a day of sitting?
Couch stretch (rear foot up on a couch, front knee 90°, hips squared). 90 seconds each side, daily. The 'feels evil' versions are the ones working.
Will do. Plant — what's a good outdoor planter that won't rot in winter?
Glazed terracotta or fibre-clay. Avoid plain unglazed terracotta (cracks in frost) and avoid plastic if it'll be UV-exposed long term.
Glazed it is. Reading — what's the strongest argument *against* universal basic income?

The substitution argument: UBI substitutes broadly-distributed cash for targeted in-kind support (housing, healthcare). For people in deep need, the targeted version is more efficient per pound. The case is sharpest where housing markets are tight: cash gets bid into rents.
Useful framing. Fashion — recommend a single belt that lasts a decade?
An Anson Calder full-grain or a Saddleback leather. £80–£200 range, both age into character rather than degradation.
Anson Calder noted. Travel — three days in Tokyo, never been, what would you prioritise?

Day 1: Yanaka + Asakusa for old Tokyo. Day 2: Shimokitazawa + Shibuya for vintage and modern. Day 3: Tsukiji outer market morning + a teamLab installation evening. Don't bother with Mt Fuji unless you have day 4.
Tokyo soon. Sleep — earlier-and-lighter dinner vs later-and-richer for sleep quality?

Earlier and lighter, by a wide margin. Late-and-rich raises core temperature during sleep onset, fragments the first sleep cycle, and pushes REM later. Three hours before bed is the conservative cutoff; four for heavy meals.
Three hours, fair. Music — a useful classical entry point for someone who only knows pop?
Try Schubert's piano trios, Bach's *Goldberg Variations* (the 1981 Gould recording), and Górecki's Symphony No. 3. Three different doors into the genre — pick the one that hits.
Górecki sounds new. Office — best way to manage too many open browser tabs?

Three rules: workspace per project (Arc browser handles this best), close anything older than 48 hours unread, and dump research links to a single read-it-later app instead of leaving them open. The last one is the load-bearing rule.
Arc, will try. Fitness — minimum effective squat rack setup for a small flat?
Wall-mount foldable rack (Rogue RML-3W or PRx) plus a 20kg bar plus 100kg of plates. Footprint is essentially a doormat when folded. Anything cheaper risks safety; anything more is gym territory.
Foldable rack, noted. Garden — soil mix for tomatoes in containers?
Two parts compost, one part topsoil, one part perlite, plus a slow-release tomato feed at planting. Water deeply less often, never lightly often.
Got it. Quick one — best app for tracking books read?
Storygraph if you want stats and recommendations beyond Goodreads's tired interface. Hardcover.app is rising. Both let you import a Goodreads CSV.
Storygraph, will switch. Last small one — a single book on negotiation worth reading?

*Never Split the Difference* by Voss is the popular pick; *Getting to Yes* (Fisher & Ury) is the classic. They cover overlapping ground; Voss is more usable on the spot, Fisher is more usable in long negotiations.
Voss, noted. Tea — how long should green tea actually steep?
60–90 seconds for sencha-style at 70–80°C. Boiling water plus 3 minutes is what makes green tea taste astringent; the steeping time matters less than the temperature.
70–80°C, will measure. Quick one — the difference between a stout and a porter?

Historically the same drink at different strengths; modern usage has drifted so porters are typically lighter and chocolate-noted while stouts lean towards roasted and full-bodied. Plenty of overlap; no firm rule any brewer is bound by.
OK back to actual work. For the analytics service decision I mentioned at the very start — what database did I go with, and what did I rule out and why?
